title: Specify group in import file

posts.author: jaydeflix (John Eddy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can appreciate the current import functionality but I’d like to request a feature improvement:

The ability to map a column from the import data to specify which Group items should be imported into.

It doesn’t need to be required, I can see the benefit of having it go into a new group, but for a more advanced experience, being able to cut out the step of moving items to the correct groups would be a time saver.

posts.author: cat.goliath (Cat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need this feature as well! All we need is the option to map one column by exact name to a group name in monday.com.

I repeatedly import an excel file into a board once a week. Currently when the item exists, it will overwrite and stay in the group I have manually moved it to, but if a new item occurs, it creates a new group by the exact name of my file.

Specifying groups would be a game changer!

posts.author: Josephine (Josephine)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would also be helpful when wanting to update a particular group of items (utilizing the override). Right now it will override all items with same identifier on the board. It would be great to be able to import directly to a group and have it only override the items in that group.

posts.author: Shadi (Shadi)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 would really love to see this feature

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml: Sign me up as well. We do a lot of importing. i get the concept, place it in a separate group in case you make a mistake. The better solution is to have an import group and use that one all the time. The constant deleting of groups of aggravating.

posts.author: Jornature (Joris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is this feature already possible somehow?
I am quite surprised that it makes new groups on import unless it is an update.
The whole import feature is quite useless if you can’t choose this right?

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jaydeflix

If you’re still looking for this, then you can import an Excel/CSV into an existing group with the monday app Data Importer

I’ve put together a tutorial specifically for this too.

I hope it helps, do feel free to reach out if any questions!

James

Screenshot 2025-01-04 at 11.52.091058×727 28.8 KB

posts.author: hackercules (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jaydeflix !

Unfortunately monday still does not provide the way to import data into existing groups.

After import you can use checkbox under group name to select [up to 500] group items and move them to the single specified group through Move to → Move to group menu.

But if your data already contains column which determines the target group then you can use my monday app Split to groups to move selected items to the designated groups.

For advanced usage application can use Formula column as group name source, so it is possible to implement your own logic to calculate correct target group.
